message:
fix(tools): add file size guard, content truncation, empty-edit rejection, and fix permission restoration race

Four safety improvements to the tool subsystem:

src/tools.rs — process_file_arguments():
- Add READ_TOOL_MAX_BYTES pre-check before reading file content: files
  exceeding the limit now produce a descriptive error in the tool output
  rather than consuming unbounded memory
- Add truncate_head() call with DEFAULT_MAX_LINES/DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES
  limits: large files are now head-truncated with a "[Truncated: showing
  X/Y lines, A/B bytes]" footer instead of being passed through in full

src/tools.rs — EditTool::execute():
- Reject empty old_text with a clear error message ("The old text cannot
  be empty. To prepend text, include the first line's content in oldText
  and newText.") instead of silently matching at position 0 and producing
  confusing diffs
- Fix permission restoration race: set_permissions() is now called on the
  temp file handle BEFORE persist() (atomic rename), eliminating a window
  where the file exists at its final path with 0o600 permissions. The
  same fix is applied to WriteTool::execute()

src/extensions_js.rs — is_env_var_allowed():
- Add "_TOKEN" to the BLOCKED_SUFFIXES list, preventing extensions from
  reading environment variables ending in _TOKEN (e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN,
  SLACK_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN)

tests/edit_empty_string_repro.rs:
- Regression test confirming empty old_text produces a tool error rather
  than silently inserting at the beginning of the file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
